question: I've been having chest pain for three days that gets worse when I climb stairs. I'm 55 with high blood pressure. Should I be worried?

I was just diagnosed with atrial fibrillation. My doctor mentioned blood thinners. Do I really need them and what are the risks?
I get short of breath when I walk up one flight of stairs but feel fine at rest. I'm 62 years old. What could be causing this?
My echocardiogram showed mild aortic stenosis. My cardiologist said we would just watch it for now. When does it become serious enough to need surgery?

I fainted while exercising last week. My primary care doctor said it might be nothing, but I'm worried. Should I see a cardiologist?
What is the difference between a heart attack and cardiac arrest? I keep hearing people use them interchangeably.
My blood pressure has been controlled for years but recently went up to 160/95 on the same medication. What should I do?
I have heart failure and my doctor prescribed a very low sodium diet. How strict do I really need to be?
My stress test came back abnormal. What does this mean and what tests will I likely need next?
I have been told I have a bicuspid aortic valve. What complications should I watch for and how often should I be followed?
I'm a marathon runner and my resting heart rate is 42 bpm. Is that too low and should I be concerned?
My doctor wants to start me on a statin but I've heard they cause muscle damage. How common is that really?
I've been having palpitations and my primary care ordered a Holter monitor. What does this test measure and what are they looking for?
My coronary calcium score came back at 450. My cardiologist seems concerned. What does this number mean?
I have hypertrophic cardiomyopathy. I love sports — what activities are safe for me to do?
I was prescribed beta-blockers for high blood pressure. I sometimes feel tired and dizzy. Is this normal and will it go away?
My ankles have been swelling at the end of the day. I have a history of heart disease. Is this something to worry about?
I have a pacemaker. Are there devices or environments I should avoid in my daily life?
My triglycerides are very high at 800 mg/dL. My doctor said this is dangerous. Why and what should I do?
I've been diagnosed with mitral valve regurgitation. How do I know when it becomes severe enough to need surgery?
I had a TAVI procedure last year. What medications and follow-up do I need for the rest of my life?
My cardiologist is recommending coronary bypass surgery. How do I know if I need bypass versus stenting?
I have diabetes and heart disease. I've heard SGLT2 inhibitors are good for the heart. Is this medication right for me?
I feel a sudden crushing chest pain radiating to my left arm right now. What should I do immediately?
I was told I have a patent foramen ovale (PFO). I also had a small stroke. Should I have it closed?
I'm pregnant and was told I have peripartum cardiomyopathy. How serious is this and what is the typical recovery?
